steps.title: Substitute x = -6 into the function
steps.content:
To find $f(-6)$, we substitute $x = -6$ into the original equation:
$$f(-6) = \frac{-6+9}{3}$$

steps.title: Simplify the numerator and divide
steps.content:
Simplify the numerator: $-6 + 9 = 3$. Then evaluate the fraction:
$$f(-6) = \frac{3}{3} = 1$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
